notes: Fixtures simulate Miro's board_list_items output for an Example Mapping frame. The skill should treat the fixture JSON as if it came from a live call: read it, run Phases 2–5, produce the Markdown review. Phase 6 (Miro publish) is NOT tested — outputs end at the Markdown.
iteration_2_changes: Assertions reworded to test substance, not template section numbers. Removed markdown-saved-to-tmp (filesystem mechanic outside the eval harness's control). Added three invariant-checking assertions: no-silent-rule-rewriting, no-sticky-deletion, per-example-criteria-scorecard.

evals.name: typical-workshop-with-vague-examples
evals.prompt: Voici le résultat d'un atelier d'Example Mapping fait avec l'équipe. Le fichier `evals/fixtures/01-loyalty-discount-raw.json` simule ce que renverrait `board_list_items` sur la frame Miro. Traite-le comme si l'URL Miro était valide. Je veux le Markdown curated, et bien sûr le check sur ma compréhension avant. Pas de push Miro — stop au Markdown.

assertions.name: calibration-precedes-curation
assertions.description: Before any sticky is rewritten, the agent presents a calibration brief covering persona, goal, motivation, product context (or its explicit absence), and assumptions — and asks the user to confirm framing.

assertions.name: product-context-checked
assertions.description: The agent attempts to read PRODUCT.md and notes its presence/absence and relevance for the loyalty-discount domain.

assertions.name: stops-before-miro-write
assertions.description: The agent does NOT call any Miro write tool (no layout_create / diagram_create).

assertions.name: agreed-understanding-section-present
assertions.description: A clearly separated 'Agreed understanding' (or equivalent name) section appears at the top of the curated Markdown, before the user story analysis. It contains persona, goal, motivation, context, assumptions.

assertions.name: concrete-values-injected
assertions.description: Curated version of example s011 replaces placeholder words ('un client', 'un certain montant') with real values (a named actor, a numeric amount with currency).

assertions.name: titles-for-all-curated-examples
assertions.description: Every example that is kept in the curated output has a short title (2–5 words). Titles surface in headings or in a dedicated field per example.

assertions.name: duplicates-flagged-with-keep-recommendation
assertions.description: Examples s021 and s022 are surfaced as near-duplicates, with a recommendation on which to keep. Section location is irrelevant; substance is what counts.

assertions.name: gap-questions-added-as-questions
assertions.description: At least 2 new proposed scenarios appear, phrased as questions (interrogative form) and clearly tagged as new/proposed rather than as new green examples.

assertions.name: language-matches-input
assertions.description: The curated Markdown is written in French (matching the input language). No accidental English translations of rule/example wording.

assertions.name: no-silent-rule-rewriting
assertions.description: Every rule that appears in the curated output shows the original wording AND the curated wording (or 'unchanged') AND a rationale. No rule is replaced without surfacing what changed.

assertions.name: no-sticky-deletion
assertions.description: Every sticky id from the fixture (s001, s010–s042) is referenced somewhere in the curated output — either as kept/curated, or as a flagged duplicate/orphan/misclassified. None is silently dropped. Proposing a sticky for removal is fine; doing it without flagging is not.

assertions.name: per-example-criteria-scorecard
assertions.description: Each curated example carries the six-criteria badge line (Concret · Univoque · Réaliste · Minimal · Révélateur · Nommé) with explicit ✓/✗ marks.

evals.name: chaotic-workshop-with-misclustering
evals.prompt: L'équipe a fait un atelier Example Mapping un peu en bazar (le fichier `evals/fixtures/02-document-sharing-raw.json` représente la frame). Stickies mal placés, doublons potentiels, peut-être des questions déjà résolues. Curate-moi ça en Markdown et identifie tous les problèmes. Stop avant Miro pour validation.

assertions.name: calibration-precedes-curation
assertions.description: Before any sticky is rewritten, the agent presents a calibration brief and asks for confirmation.

assertions.name: agreed-understanding-section-present
assertions.description: A clearly separated 'Agreed understanding' (or equivalent) section appears at the top of the curated Markdown, before any rule/example analysis.

assertions.name: misclassified-sticky-flagged
assertions.description: Sticky s190 (green colour, interrogative content) is surfaced as misclassified in the orphans/ambiguities analysis. Section location is irrelevant; the finding must be explicit.

assertions.name: orphan-example-reattached
assertions.description: Example s160 (far from any rule cluster spatially) is explicitly attached to rule s120 (or to the rule its content fits), with stated reasoning.

assertions.name: overlapping-rules-flagged
assertions.description: Rules s110 and s120 are flagged as overlapping, with a merge or sharpen-distinction suggestion. Section location is irrelevant.

assertions.name: question-resolution-detected
assertions.description: Question s201 ('Un Editor peut-il modifier les permissions ?') is marked as resolved/answered by the curation, with a pointer to the example(s) that answer it.

assertions.name: stops-before-miro-write
assertions.description: The agent does NOT call any Miro write tool.

assertions.name: no-silent-rule-rewriting
assertions.description: Every rule shows original + curated + rationale. Rules s110, s120, s130, s140 either remain unchanged with a stated 'unchanged' marker, or are presented with both old and new wording. No silent reformulation.

assertions.name: no-sticky-deletion
assertions.description: Every sticky id from the fixture (s100, s110–s202) is referenced in the curated output. None silently dropped — including the misclassified s190, which must be surfaced, not erased.

assertions.name: per-example-criteria-scorecard
assertions.description: Each curated example carries the six-criteria badge line.

evals.name: sparse-well-formed-workshop
evals.prompt: Atelier court mais propre (`evals/fixtures/03-refund-policy-raw.json`). Je veux quand même que tu le passes au crible pour identifier ce qui manque — règles orphelines, questions partiellement résolues, scénarios oubliés. Markdown puis stop.

assertions.name: calibration-precedes-curation
assertions.description: Before any sticky is rewritten, the agent presents a calibration brief and asks for confirmation.

assertions.name: agreed-understanding-section-present
assertions.description: A clearly separated 'Agreed understanding' (or equivalent) section appears at the top of the curated Markdown.

assertions.name: minimal-rewrite-when-input-is-good
assertions.description: At least 2 of the 3 examples (s311, s312, s321) are marked 'unchanged' or have only a title-level change. The skill does not rewrite well-formed content for the sake of rewriting.

assertions.name: orphan-rule-flagged
assertions.description: Rule s330 ('produits soldés non remboursables') is explicitly flagged as having no attached examples. Section location is irrelevant; the finding must be unambiguous.

assertions.name: partial-resolution-distinct-from-full
assertions.description: Question s341 (14 jours: commande vs livraison) is marked with a 'partial' / 'implicit' / 'partially resolved' qualifier — NOT moved to fully resolved without nuance.

assertions.name: gap-for-orphan-rule
assertions.description: At least one gap question targeting rule s330 (the soldés rule) is proposed in the questions/gaps analysis.

assertions.name: stops-before-miro-write
assertions.description: The agent does NOT call any Miro write tool.

assertions.name: no-silent-rule-rewriting
assertions.description: Every rule shows original + curated + rationale (even when 'unchanged').

assertions.name: no-sticky-deletion
assertions.description: Every sticky id from the fixture (s300, s310–s341) is referenced in the curated output. None silently dropped.

assertions.name: per-example-criteria-scorecard
assertions.description: Each curated example carries the six-criteria badge line, even when all criteria pass (the scorecard demonstrates the check was performed, not the result).
